caption: Water temperature, surface vs. bottom — Northern & Southern sites. Surface peaks ~28 °C in August; the bottom stays near 6 °C, showing strong summer stratification.
Total phosphorus — surface stays low (“Excellent”) while bottom phosphorus climbs late-season, peaking 0.408 mg/L at the Southern site.
Sediment plumes from the Route 9 stormwater outfall during Belmont Street construction — total phosphorus reached 4.66 mg/L at the outfall.
Dissolved oxygen — surface “Excellent” all season, bottom water hypoxic (below 4 mg/L) after late May.
Mapped invasive aquatic-plant cover. Eurasian Milfoil and Fanwort are densest in the southern lake and Flint Pond.
Regatta Point Beach Enterococcus results. Single-sample bars against the 61 CFU/100 mL threshold; peak ~228 on Aug 13.
Depth profiles of temperature and dissolved oxygen — the oxycline shoals to 25–30 ft by late July, compressing cold-water fish habitat.
Secchi-depth water clarity, Northern & Southern sites — 7.25–14.75 ft across the season, most readings in the “Fair” band.
Total phosphorus 2025 — bottom phosphorus rose sharply to a record 0.673 mg/L at the Southern site.
August 2025 aquatic-plant cover survey, confirming the 2023 herbicide treatment success (Eurasian Milfoil reduced to two small patches).
